commit.message:
fix: signal WS subscribers when TransactionFinalized received from network

When a masternode receives a TransactionFinalized broadcast from a peer,
it was correctly moving the tx to the finalized pool but never calling
signal_tx_finalized(). This meant wallets subscribed to that node would
never receive the utxo_finalized WS event, leaving received transactions
stuck in Pending state indefinitely.

Fix: call consensus.signal_tx_finalized(*txid) after processing the
TransactionFinalized network message, consistent with the other two
finalization paths (local auto-finalization and TimeVote accumulation).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: hub-rank scores on illuminate results (1.0=most central → 0)

All illuminate: results previously returned score=1.0 — meaningless to
both humans ('lol all the same') and LLMs (can't prioritize by relevance).

illuminate-global now assigns a normalized hub-rank score:
  score = (N - rank_index) / N
  - score 1.0  → most connected compound in the corpus
  - score ~0   → least connected in the returned set
  - Results sorted descending by score so order matches centrality

Results are also re-sorted by score so the YAML output presents the
most structurally important content first.

UI: score: n.score || 1 → n.score ?? 1 to avoid clobbering a valid
score of 0 for the least-central node.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

In GMS2 (Deltarune), tiles in Compatibility_Tiles layers have a null

instead, pointing to a UndertaleSprite named 'bg_*'.

* fix(gms2): use GMS2TileWidth/Height for native tile layer tilesets

GMS2 Background resources store explicit tile dimensions (GMS2TileWidth /
GMS2TileHeight). Previously determine_tile_dims() divided the tileset
texture size by the room's tile count (tiles_x / tiles_y), producing
wrong values (e.g. 12x34 instead of 20x20 for bg_towntiles_tileset).

Changes:
- extract_data.csx: export gms2TileWidth/gms2TileHeight for Data.Backgrounds
- schema.rs: add gms2_tile_width/gms2_tile_height fields to BackgroundDef
- convert.rs: use these fields directly when non-zero, skipping the
  broken tile-count division heuristic

fix: replace dynamic imports with static imports to fix asset pipeline

The webview message handlers in useExtensionMessages.ts used dynamic
import() calls (e.g. import('../office/sprites/assetLoader.js').then(...))
to lazily load asset-management modules. Vite code-splits these into
separate chunks whose module-level state is isolated from the main
bundle. For example, calling setWallSprites on the chunk's copy of
wallTiles.ts had NO effect on the copy used by renderer.ts (imported
statically). This caused ALL three rendering pipelines to silently fail:

1. Character sprite sheets — loadCharacterSheetsFromUris stored sheets
   in the chunk's characterSheets Map, not the renderer's copy
2. Tileset metadata — setTilesetMetadata stored the PNG image in the
   chunk's tilesetMetadataImage, so drawTilesetTile always returned
   false (plain beige/tan floor fallback)
3. Legacy tileset data — setLegacyTilesetAssets wrote to the chunk's
   tilesetData, so drawTilesetFurniture always returned false (gray
   rectangle furniture fallback)

Changes:
- Convert all dynamic import() to static import in useExtensionMessages
- Add try/catch around removeBackground() in character sheet loading
- Add connect-src to CSP so fetch()-based asset loading can work
- Set Vite emptyOutDir: true to prevent stale code-split chunks

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Scribe: Document CSP race condition diagnostic and fix

Lisa Simpson diagnosed and fixed a critical race condition in webview asset loading:
- User reported F5 testing shows default colored rectangles instead of custom sprites
- Root cause: loadAssets() used fetch() which is blocked by VS Code CSP (no connect-src)
- The catch block reset assetsReady=false, overriding the true already set by URI-based loaders
- Solution: Removed eager loadAssets() call and guarded catch block

Files modified in webview:
- webview-ui/src/office/components/OfficeCanvas.tsx (removed loadAssets call)
- webview-ui/src/office/sprites/assetLoader.ts (guarded catch block)

New Decision § 16: Never use fetch() in webview — CSP blocks it.
All asset loading must use extension-provided URIs via postMessage.

commit.message:
fix: remove eager loadAssets() call that reset assetsReady flag

OfficeCanvas.tsx called loadAssets() on mount, which used fetch()
to load tileset.json via relative URLs. This always failed because
the webview CSP has default-src 'none' with no connect-src directive.
The catch block then set assetsReady = false, overriding the true
value set by URI-based loaders (tilesetMetadataLoaded,
characterAssetsLoaded messages from the extension host).

Fix: Remove the eager loadAssets() call from OfficeCanvas since the
extension host sends webview-safe URIs for all PNG assets. Also guard
the catch block in loadAssets() so it only resets assetsReady if no
URI-based loader has already succeeded.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: YAML copy — illuminate copies all results with accurate stats

illuminate: results are pre-budget-trimmed server-side (1000 nodes,
corpus-proportional char budget). Copying only 20 of 1000 discarded
98% of the returned context. Stats footer was also wrong: it showed
tokenBudget*4 (the search slider) divided by 20 as chars/result —
completely unrelated to actual illuminate content.

- Detect illuminate mode via _algo flag on result objects
- illuminate: copy ALL results (no MAX_RESULTS cap), no per-result truncation
- Search: keep MAX_RESULTS=20 + token budget truncation (unchanged)
- Footer for illuminate: actual avg chars/result + actual total chars
- Footer for search: unchanged (token budget, capped count)

fix: cap WASM result rows in fetchContentAtomsByHubs (stack overflow)

Large corpora with 200 hub compounds × 100+ molecules each would return
10,000+ rows through a single PGlite query. Even though only IDs were
selected (~20 bytes/row), 10K rows ≈ 200KB marshaled through WASM stack
— enough to trigger 'Maximum call stack size exceeded'.

Two-part fix:
1. Add PGLITE_CHUNK_RESULT_IDS = 500 — max rows returned per fan-out
   ID query (distinct from CHUNK_IDS which caps IN-param count).
2. Per-chunk LIMIT = min(remaining, PGLITE_CHUNK_RESULT_IDS) so each
   query returns at most 500 ID rows (~10KB), iterating chunks until
   maxCount is reached.

Result: illuminate-global on a 370K+ atom corpus will iterate safely
in ~20 chunks of 500 IDs each to hit maxNodes=10000.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: adaptive status polling — 3s when ingesting, 30s when idle

Commit 978d7eb merged status poll (was 2s) + stats poll (was 10s) into
a single 30s interval to reduce overhead. This caused the ingestion
progress badge in the header to not appear for up to 30 seconds after
ingestion started — essentially invisible.

Fix: adaptive interval. Initial fetch is immediate. When isBusy changes
false→true, reschedule at 3s. When isBusy changes true→false, reschedule
at 30s. This keeps the idle overhead low while restoring real-time
ingestion feedback.

fix: render floor/wall tiles from tileset PNG instead of colored rectangles

renderTileGrid() never used the tileset PNG for floor/wall tiles — it
always drew walls as solid HSB-colored rectangles and floors as
colorized inline sprites. The tileset image was loaded correctly via
the asset pipeline but only used for furniture rendering.

Added drawTilesetTile() in tilesetRenderer.ts that maps TileType enum
values to tileset-metadata.json item IDs (FLOOR_1 → floor_wood,
FLOOR_2 → floor_blue_diamond, WALL → wall_white_panel). Updated
renderTileGrid() to try tileset rendering first, falling back to
colored rectangles when assets aren't loaded.

commit.message:
fix: illuminate uses compound_id not tags table for content pull

Root cause: tags table only contains tag stub rows (atom_id = tag_atom_id).
Content atom tags live in atoms.tags (JSON column), not the tags table.
Querying tags WHERE tag IN (...) JOIN atoms WHERE source != atom_source
returned 0 rows because every matching row IS a tag atom.

New approach: content atoms have compound_id pointing to their parent
mem_ compound. Query atoms WHERE compound_id IN (<top_hubs>) directly.

Changes:
- Replace fetchContentAtomsByTopTags with fetchContentAtomsByHubs(hubIds)
- Illuminate path skips edge-BFS entirely — goes straight hub → content
- Hub count = seed_count × 20 (default 200 hubs) capped at 500
- Atoms returned in hub-rank order then start_byte (document order)
- Budget trim still applies to limit final output

fix: exclude tag stubs from illuminate content pull via atoms JOIN

Tag atoms (source_path='atom_source') self-tag with their own label,
so they appear in the tags table and were being returned as results.

The JS filter !id.startsWith('atom_source') was wrong — that checks
the ID prefix, not the source_path field. All atom IDs start with 'atom_'.

Fix: JOIN atoms ON a.source_path != 'atom_source' in the tags query,
filtering stubs at the SQL level before they enter the score map.
